Test: Kruskal-Wallis AUC across algorithms (RandomForest, LogisticRegression, GradientBoosting, SVM)
Interpretation: Algorithms differ statistically, but the practical AUC range is 0.0274 and the best mean AUC is 0.5629.

Test: Paired Wilcoxon: CLASS-LAG AUC@10% vs AUC@100% (95 valid cell lines)
Interpretation: If AD filtering rescued the model, the top-confidence subset would show a large AUC gain.

Test: Pearson r: cell-line sample size vs mean AUC (108 cell lines)
Interpretation: Tests whether larger cell-line datasets have higher AUC.

Test: Pearson r: percent sensitive vs mean AUC (108 cell lines)
Interpretation: Tests whether class balance explains AUC variation.
Compares AUC in balanced and imbalanced cell lines.

Test: One-sample t-test: per-cell-line mean AUC vs 0.5
Interpretation: AUC is threshold-independent; a low AUC indicates weak ranking, not only poor calibration.

Test: Best CONS-STD AUC coverage vs 100% coverage
Interpretation: Checks whether selecting compounds with stronger consensus agreement improves AUC.

Test: Additive variance decomposition of Z-score matrix
Interpretation: Cell-line identity explains 4.8x more variance than drug identity.

Hypothesis: H4 - Feature insufficiency (structural vs response, AVALON)
Test: Spearman r: pairwise Tanimoto (AVALON) vs pairwise response correlation (n=5000 drug pairs)
Interpretation: r^2=0.92%: AVALON Tanimoto explains little response-profile similarity.

Hypothesis: H4 - Feature insufficiency (structural vs response, ECFP4)
Test: Spearman r: pairwise Tanimoto (ECFP4) vs pairwise response correlation (n=5000 drug pairs)
Interpretation: r^2=0.18%: ECFP4 Tanimoto explains little response-profile similarity.

Hypothesis: H4 - Feature insufficiency (structural vs response, MACCS)
Test: Spearman r: pairwise Tanimoto (MACCS) vs pairwise response correlation (n=5000 drug pairs)
Interpretation: r^2=0.42%: MACCS Tanimoto explains little response-profile similarity.

Test: Mean variance of drug response across 108 cell lines
Interpretation: High within-drug variance means the same drug behaves differently across cell-line contexts.
